html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 康王谷 - Specific location on Mount Lu, named after King Kang of Zhou.
  • 巅事类赋注引作北岭 - Reference to the mountain peak, with an additional note in the *Shi Leifu Zhi* that it is called North Ridge.
  • 一城，号为钊城 - A city named 'Zhaocun'.
  • 天每欲雨，辄闻山上鼓角笳箫之声 - Meteorological phenomenon where the sound of drums, horns, flutes, and pipes is heard before rain.
  • 屯人以为常候 - Local people consider this sound a regular sign of impending rain.
  • 此周康王之城 - This is the city of King Kang of Zhou.
  • 康王爱奇好异，巡历名山，不远而至 - King Kang of Zhou was fond of unusual things and traveled to famous mountains, not far away.
  • 城中每得古器、大鼎，及弓、弩、金之属 - The city often finds ancient artifacts, large tripods, and weapons like bows, crossbows, and gold.
  • 知非常人之所处也 - It is known that this is not a place inhabited by ordinary people.
  • 而山有“康王”之号，城又以“钊”为称，斯言将有征 - The mountain is called 'Kangwang' and the city 'Zhaocun,' suggesting that these names have historical significance.
  • 三石梁 - Three stone bridges on Mount Lu.
  • 长数十丈，广不盈尺 - Each bridge is tens of meters long but less than a foot wide.
  • 俯眄杳然无底 - When looked down upon, they appear to have no bottom.
  • 咸康中，江州刺史庾亮，迎吴猛 - During the Xiankang period, Governor Yu of Jiangzhou welcomed Wu Meng.
  • 猛将弟子煮山游观，因过此梁 - Wu Meng and his disciples visited the mountain and passed over these bridges.
  • 见一老公，坐桂树下，以玉杯承甘露，与猛，猛遍与弟子 - They saw an old man sitting under a ginkgo tree, holding a jade cup filled with sweet dew, which he gave to Wu Meng, who then distributed it to his disciples.
  • 又进至一处，见崇台广厦，玉宇金房，琳琅焜耀，晖彩眩目 - They also saw a grand terrace with wide halls, jade palaces and golden rooms, dazzling with precious stones and brilliant colors.
  • 多珍宝玉器，不可识名 - There were many precious jade and gold vessels, whose names were unknown.
  • 见数人与猛共言，若旧相识 - They saw several people talking with Wu Meng, as if they were old acquaintances.
  • 设玉膏终日 - They spent the whole day with the jade ointment.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 康王 - King Kang of Zhou, a historical figure.
  • 钊城 - The city of Zhaocun, named after King Kang.
  • 咸康 - The reign title of Emperor Mu of Jin during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 江州 - A region in southern China.
  • 庾亮 - A prominent official during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 吴猛 - A famous Taoist sage and magician.
  • 崇台广厦 - Refers to grand and luxurious buildings.
  • 玉宇金房 - Describes palaces made of jade and gold, symbolizing luxury and opulence.
  • 琳琅焜耀 - Describes precious stones and jewels that shine brightly.
  • 晖彩眩目 - Describes the brilliance and dazzle of the colors.
  • 玉膏 - Jade ointment, a metaphor for something precious and rare.

Proper Nouns

  • 康王谷 - Specific location on Mount Lu.
  • 钊城 - The city of Zhaocun.
  • 咸康 - The reign title of Emperor Mu of Jin.
  • 江州 - A region in southern China.
  • 庾亮 - A prominent official during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 吴猛 - A famous Taoist sage and magician.
  • 崇台广厦 - Grand and luxurious buildings.
  • 玉宇金房 - Palaces made of jade and gold.
  • 琳琅焜耀 - Precious stones and jewels that shine brightly.
  • 晖彩眩目 - Brilliant and dazzling colors.
  • 玉膏 - Jade ointment.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 昔有人发庐山采松 - Formerly, there was a person who went to Mount Lu to gather pine trees.
  • 闻人语云 - Heard someone say.
  • 此未可取 - This cannot be taken.
  • 掇而服之 - Picked it up and consumed it.
  • 得寿三百岁也 - Gained a lifespan of three hundred years.
  • 形甚可爱 - Very lovely or extremely charming.
  • 其香非常 - Extraordinary or unusual fragrance.
  • 知是神异 - Recognized it as divine or supernatural.
  • 因掇而服之 - Therefore, picked it up and consumed it.
  • 得寿三百岁也 - Gained a lifespan of three hundred years.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 庐山 - Mount Lu, a famous mountain in Jiangxi Province known for its scenic beauty and historical significance.
  • 珠林 - A collection of stories or a book, often containing tales of the supernatural or miraculous.
  • 南康 - A historical region in Jiangxi Province known for its rich cultural heritage and natural beauty.
  • 东望山 - Dongwang Mountain, a specific location in the region of Nankang.

Proper Nouns

  • 庐山 - Mount Lu, a famous mountain in Jiangxi Province.
  • 珠林 - A collection of stories or a book, often containing tales of the supernatural.
  • 南康 - A historical region in Jiangxi Province.
  • 东望山 - Dongwang Mountain, a specific location in the region of Nankang.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 形如人，长二尺余 - Literal meaning: 'It has a human-like form, about two feet long.'
  • 内甚泽，五色鲜明 - Literal meaning: 'Inside is very smooth and brightly colored in five hues.'
  • 二枚沓之，中央相连 - Literal meaning: 'Two pieces are layered together, connected in the middle.'
  • 体质虚轻，颇似木筒 - Literal meaning: 'Its body is light and hollow, somewhat resembling a wooden tube.'
  • 中央以鸟毛为褥 - Literal meaning: 'In the center, it is lined with bird feathers.'
  • 此神能变化隐身 - Literal meaning: 'This deity can change and hide itself.'
  • 罕广记引作卒睹其状 - Literal meaning: 'Rarely, according to the broad records, can one suddenly see its form.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南康 - A historical region in Jiangxi Province, China.
  • 山都 - A mythical creature or deity in Chinese folklore.
  • 宋元嘉元年 - The first year of the Yuanjia era of the Song Dynasty (424 AD).
  • 赣县 - An ancient county in present-day Jiangxi Province.
  • 余公塘 - An ancient pond named after a person named Yuguang.
  • 袁道训道虚 - Historical figures, likely brothers from the county.
  • 御览八百八十四广记三百二十四 - References to historical records and encyclopedias.

Proper Nouns

  • 南康 - A historical region in Jiangxi Province, China.
  • 山都 - A mythical creature or deity in Chinese folklore.
  • 宋元嘉元年 - The first year of the Yuanjia era of the Song Dynasty (424 AD).
  • 赣县 - An ancient county in present-day Jiangxi Province.
  • 余公塘 - An ancient pond named after a person named Yuguang.
  • 袁道训道虚 - Historical figures, likely brothers from the county.
  • 御览八百八十四广记三百二十四 - References to historical records and encyclopedias.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 南康雩都縣沿江西出 - Describes the geographical feature of Yudu County in Nankang, where the river flows westward.
  • 名夢口 - Names the location as 'Mengkou' (Dream Mouth).
  • 有穴、狀如石室 - Describes a cave that looks like a stone chamber.
  • 舊傳 - Refers to an old tradition or legend.
  • 色如好金 - Describes the color of the chicken as resembling fine gold.
  • 奮翼迥翔長鳴 - Describes the chicken spreading its wings, flying far, and making a long cry.
  • 響見之，辄飛入穴中 - Describes the sound of the chicken being heard, and it immediately flies into the cave.
  • 號此石為金雞石 - Names the stone as 'Golden Chicken Stone.'
  • 略見類聚九十 - Indicates that the text is briefly mentioned in the *Leeguji* (类聚) volume 90.
  • 昔有人耕此山側 - Describes someone plowing the side of the mountain.
  • 望見雞出遊戲 - Describes seeing the chicken come out to play.
  • 有一長人操彈彈之 - Describes a tall person holding a slingshot and aiming at the chicken.
  • 彈丸正着穴上 - Describes the pebble hitting the top of the cave.
  • 丸徑六尺許 - Describes the pebble as about six feet in diameter.
  • 下垂蔽穴 - Describes the pebble hanging down and covering the cave.
  • 猶有间隙 - Describes there still being a gap.
  • 不復容人 - Describes the gap not being large enough for a person to pass through.
  • 又有人乘船从下流还县 - Describes someone traveling upstream by boat.
  • 未至此崖数里 - Describes the person not yet reaching the cliff several miles away.
  • 有一人通身黄衣 - Describes a person wearing a yellow robe.
  • 担两笼黄瓜 - Describes the person carrying two baskets of cucumbers.
  • 求寄载 - Describes the person asking to be transported.
  • 黄衣人乞食 - Describes the person in the yellow robe asking for food.
  • 船主与之盘酒 - Describes the boat owner giving him a plate of food and wine.
  • 食讫 - Describes the person finishing eating.
  • 船适至崖下 - Describes the boat arriving at the base of the cliff.
  • 船主乞瓜 - Describes the boat owner asking for cucumbers.
  • 此人不与 - Describes the person not giving the cucumbers.
  • 仍唾盘上 - Describes the person spitting on the plate.
  • 径上崖，直入石中 - Describes the person climbing up the cliff and entering the stone.
  • 船主初甚忿之 - Describes the boat owner initially being very angry.
  • 见其入石 - Describes seeing the person enter the stone.
  • 始知神异 - Describes realizing the person was supernatural.
  • 取向食器视之 - Describes taking the plate to look at it.
  • 见盘上唾，悉是黄金 - Describes seeing the spittle on the plate turn into gold.
  • 珠林二十八广记四百御览八百十一又三百八十七事类赋注九 - Indicates that the text is cited in *Zhulin* (珠林) volume 28, *Tang Huiyao* (唐会要) volume 400, *Yulu* (御览) volumes 811 and 387, and *Shilie Fu Zhu* (事类赋注) volume 9.
  • 案亦见今本任昉述异记然甚简略不如此文详尽 - Indicates that the text is also found in *Ren Fang Shuyi Ji* (任昉述异记) but is much simpler and less detailed than this text.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南康雩都县 - Yudu County in Nankang, a place in southern China.
  • 广记 - *Tang Huiyao* (唐会要), a historical record from the Tang Dynasty.
  • 类聚 - *Leeguji* (类聚), a collection of historical records.
  • 珠林 - *Zhulin* (珠林), a collection of historical records.
  • 御览 - *Yulu* (御览), a collection of historical records.
  • 事类赋注 - *Shilie Fu Zhu* (事类赋注), a commentary on the *Shilie Fu* (事类赋).
  • 任昉述异记 - *Ren Fang Shuyi Ji* (任昉述异记), a collection of strange stories by Ren Fang.
  • 金鸡石 - Golden Chicken Stone, a legendary stone.
  • 梦口穴 - Mengkou Cave, a legendary cave.
  • 神鸡 - Divine chicken, a legendary creature.
  • 长人 - Tall person, a supernatural figure.
  • 黄衣人 - Person in yellow robe, a supernatural figure.
  • 黄瓜 - Cucumbers, a common vegetable.
  • 盘酒 - Plate of food and wine, a common offering.
  • 唾盘上 - Spitting on the plate, a supernatural act.
  • 黄金 - Gold, a precious metal.

Proper Nouns

  • 南康雩都县 - Yudu County in Nankang.
  • 广记 - *Tang Huiyao* (唐会要).
  • 类聚 - *Leeguji* (类聚).
  • 珠林 - *Zhulin* (珠林).
  • 御览 - *Yulu* (御览).
  • 事类赋注 - *Shilie Fu Zhu* (事类赋注).
  • 任昉述异记 - *Ren Fang Shuyi Ji* (任昉述异记).
  • 金鸡石 - Golden Chicken Stone.
  • 梦口穴 - Mengkou Cave.
  • 神鸡 - Divine chicken.
  • 长人 - Tall person.
  • 黄衣人 - Person in yellow robe.
  • 黄瓜 - Cucumbers.
  • 盘酒 - Plate of food and wine.
  • 唾盘上 - Spitting on the plate.
  • 黄金 - Gold.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 芦塘有鲛鱼 - Literally means 'There is a mermaid in the reed pond,' but in this context, it refers to a mythical creature.
  • 五日一化 - Means 'transforms every five days,' referring to the periodic transformation of the creature.
  • 或为美异妇人，或为男子 - Means 'sometimes it transforms into a beautiful and extraordinary woman, sometimes into a man,' describing the creature's ability to change form.
  • 至于变乱尤多 - Means 'and the transformations are particularly numerous,' indicating the variety of forms the creature can take.
  • 郡人相戒 - Means 'the people of the county warn each other,' suggesting a communal effort to avoid harm.
  • 鲛亦不能为计 - Means 'the mermaid cannot plan against them,' implying that the creature is helpless against human warnings.
  • 后为雷电杀之 - Means 'later, it was killed by lightning,' indicating the creature's demise.
  • 此塘遂涸 - Means 'and this pond dried up,' suggesting the aftermath of the creature's death.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 芦塘 - Refers to a reed pond, a common feature in Chinese landscapes.
  • 鲛鱼 - Refers to a mythical mermaid or fish-human hybrid, often associated with water and transformation in Chinese folklore.
  • 雷电 - Refers to thunder and lightning, natural phenomena often associated with divine power and destruction in Chinese mythology.
  • 御览 - Refers to the *Taiping Guangji* (太平广记), a comprehensive collection of stories and anecdotes from various sources, compiled during the Song Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 御览 - The *Taiping Guangji* (太平广记), a comprehensive collection of stories and anecdotes from various sources, compiled during the Song Dynasty.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 豫章郡 - Refers to Yuzhang Commandery, an administrative region in ancient China.
  • 盧松屯 - A specific location within Yuzhang Commandery.
  • 羅根生 - A person's name, possibly a local figure.
  • 新板墨書 - Refers to a newly written board with black ink.
  • 神地所游处 - Means 'the place where the deity roams.'
  • 种殖可速去 - Means 'you may cultivate quickly and leave.'
  • 朱书赐报 - Means 'to write in red ink as a favor.'
  • 寒石山 - A specific mountain.
  • 家舅所住 - Means 'the place where my maternal uncle resides.'
  • 某且往寄憩 - Means 'I will go to stay temporarily.'
  • 鸣鞞响角 - Refers to the sound of drums and horns.
  • 淩空而逝 - Means 'to pass through the air and depart.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 豫章郡 - A historical administrative region in present-day Jiangxi Province.
  • 白道猷 - A foreigner who lived in China during the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
  • 赤城 - A specific mountain in the region.
  • 晋泰元中 - Refers to the Taiyuan period of the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
  • 夏王之子 - Refers to a son of the legendary Xia Dynasty king.
  • 寒石山 - A specific mountain associated with the deity.
  • 会稽山庙 - Refers to a temple in Kuaiji Mountain, a famous site in Zhejiang Province.

Proper Nouns

  • 豫章郡 - Yuzhang Commandery.
  • 卢松屯 - Lu Song Encampment.
  • 罗根生 - Luo Gensheng.
  • 赤城 - Red City Mountain.
  • 白道猷 - Bai Daoyou.
  • 寒石山 - Hanzhi Mountain.
  • 会稽山庙 - Kuaiji Mountain Temple.
  • 晋泰元中 - Taiyuan period of the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
  • 夏王之子 - Son of the Xia Dynasty king.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 沦为湖 - Literally 'became a lake,' indicating a geographical transformation.
  • 此县门石龟眼血出 - Cryptic warning, 'The stone turtle's eyes on the gate of this county bleeded.'
  • 此地当陷为湖 - Prophecy, 'This place will sink into a lake.'
  • 姥具以告 - Indicates the old woman's actions, 'The old woman fully informed.'
  • 吏遂以朱点龟眼 - Symbolic act, 'The official then marked the turtle's eyes with red.'
  • 遂走上北山 - Key part of the old woman's escape, 'She then ran up the north mountain.'
  • 城遂陷 - Fulfillment of the prophecy, 'The city then sank.'
  • 出海口北行六十里 - Geographical description, 'Traveling sixty li north from the mouth of the river.'
  • 至腾屿之南溪 - Specific location, 'Reaching the southern stream of Teng Island.'
  • 有淡水，清澈照底 - Describes the water, 'There is fresh water, clear enough to see the bottom.'
  • 有蟹焉 - Introduces the crabs, 'There are crabs there.'
  • 筐大如笠 - Descriptive phrase, 'The crab's shell is as large as a bamboo hat.'
  • 脚长三足 - Descriptive phrase, 'Its legs are three feet long.'
  • 肥美过常 - Describes the taste, 'It was unusually delicious.'
  • 虎其夜梦一少妪语之曰 - Dream sequence, 'The tiger dreamt of a young woman speaking to it.'
  • 汝啖我，知汝寻被啖不 - Prophecy, 'You ate me; do you know you will soon be eaten?'
  • 屠氏明日出行 - Continues the narrative, 'The next day, the tiger went out.'
  • 为虎所食 - Fulfillment of the prophecy, 'Was eaten by the tiger.'
  • 余家人殡瘗之 - Describes the burial, 'His family buried him.'
  • 虎又发棺啖之 - Supernatural event, 'The tiger dug up the coffin and ate him.'
  • 肌体无遗 - Final outcome, 'No remains were left.'
  • 此水今犹有大蟹 - Current state, 'There are still large crabs in this water.'
  • 莫敢复犯 - Warning and conclusion, 'No one dares to touch them again.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 和州历阳 - Region in ancient China, likely referring to a specific area that became submerged.
  • 类林杂说十 - Source of the text, 'Miscellaneous notes on various topics, volume ten.'
  • 宋元嘉中 - Historical context, 'During the Yuanchi era of the Song Dynasty.'
  • 章安县 - County in ancient China, likely referring to a specific location.

Proper Nouns

  • 和州历阳 - Region in ancient China.
  • 类林杂说十 - Source of the text, 'Miscellaneous notes on various topics, volume ten.'
  • 宋元嘉中 - Historical context, 'During the Yuanchi era of the Song Dynasty.'
  • 章安县 - County in ancient China.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 園客種五色香草 - Literal description of a man named Yuan Ke planting five-colored fragrant grasses.
  • 有五色蛾集其上 - Describes five-colored moths gathering on the grass, a vivid and symbolic image.
  • 得茧百二十枚，大如瓮 - Specific and striking detail about the size of the cocoons.
  • 女与客俱仙去 - Fantastical element indicating that both the woman and Yuan Ke ascended to immortality.
  • 朱翌倚觉寮杂记上 - Reference to the source of the text, indicating it is from a collection of miscellaneous notes.
  • 汉宣城太守封邵忽化为虎，食郡民 - Story of a governor transforming into a tiger and eating the people of his jurisdiction.
  • 民呼曰封使君，因去不复来 - Narrative of the people calling him 'Governor Feng' and he leaving permanently.
  • 时语曰：“无作封使君，生不治民死食民” - Proverb warning against becoming a governor like Feng.
  • 海录碎事十二 - Reference to the source of the text, indicating it is from a collection of miscellaneous records.
  • 吴黄龙年中，吴都海盐有陆东美，妻朱氏 - Specific time and place in the story of Lu Dongmei and his wife Zhu.
  • 亦有容止，夫妻相重，寸步不相离 - Describes the couple's deep affection and constant companionship.
  • 时人号为“比肩人” - The couple was called 'Shoulder-to-Shoulder People.'
  • 夫妇云皆比翼，恐不能佳也 - The couple believed that being 'winged' (a metaphor for being inseparable) might not be as good.
  • 后妻死，东美不食求死，家人哀之，乃合葬 - After his wife's death, Lu Dongmei refused to eat and sought to die, but his family mourned and buried them together.
  • 未一岁，冢上生梓树，同根二身，相抱而合成一树 - Within a year, a pair of willow trees grew from the same root and embraced each other.
  • 每有双鸿，常宿于上 - A pair of cranes often perched on the tree.
  • 孙权闻之嗟叹，封其里曰“比肩墓”，又曰“双梓” - Sun Quan heard of this and named the area 'Shoulder-to-Shoulder Tomb' and 'Double Willow.'
  • 后子弘与妻张氏，虽无异，亦相爱慕 - Later, their son Hong and his wife Zhang were also deeply affectionate.
  • 吴人又呼为“小比肩” - The people of Wu called them 'Little Shoulder-to-Shoulder People.'
  • 广记三百八十九 - Reference to the source of the text, indicating it is from a collection of miscellaneous records.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 園客 - A legendary figure known for his cultivation of fragrant grasses and his ascension to immortality.
  • 漢宣城太守封邵 - A historical figure who became a tiger and ate the people of his jurisdiction.
  • 吳黃龍年中 - Refers to the year 231 AD during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 吳都海鹽 - Refers to the city of Haiyan in the Wu kingdom.
  • 陸東美 - A historical figure known for his deep love for his wife.
  • 孫權 - The ruler of the Wu kingdom during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 比肩人 - A term used to describe couples who are inseparable and deeply affectionate.
  • 雙梓 - A term used to describe the willow trees that grew from the same root.

Proper Nouns

  • 園客 - A legendary figure.
  • 封邵 - A historical figure.
  • 孫權 - The ruler of the Wu kingdom.
  • 陸東美 - A historical figure.
  • 朱氏 - Lu Dongmei's wife.
  • 張氏 - Lu Hong's wife.
  • 比肩人 - A term used to describe couples who are inseparable and deeply affectionate.
  • 雙梓 - A term used to describe the willow trees that grew from the same root.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 颇好游猎 - Quite fond of hunting or hunting expeditions.
  • 黠慧能解人语 - Clever and intelligent, can understand human speech.
  • 识路自还 - Know the way, return by itself.
  • 赍书驰取消息 - Carry a letter, run to fetch news.
  • 弭耳掉尾 - Lower the ears, wag the tail.
  • 腾上 - Jump onto or climb onto.
  • 口衔筒作声示之 - Mouth holding the bamboo tube, make a sound to show it.
  • 殡之 - To bury it.
  • 遣送还葬 - To send, back to the burial place.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 陆机 - Lu Ji (261–303), a prominent literary figure of the Western Jin dynasty, known for his poetry and prose.
  • 黄耳 - The name of the dog, which is a proper noun in this context.
  • 吴 - Refers to the state of Wu during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 洛 - Refers to Luoyang, the capital of the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 屯南 - Refers to a military camp or settlement, south.

Proper Nouns

  • 陆机 - Lu Ji, a prominent literary figure of the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 黄耳 - The name of the dog.
  • 吴 - The state of Wu during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 洛 - Luoyang, the capital of the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 黄耳冢 - The name of the dog's grave.

[10]
